subject: Business Studies
source_note: This JSON contains questions 1-18 only, as the PDF source document includes only pages covering Q1-Q18 of the 2022 paper. Questions 19-50 were not present in the provided PDF.
instructions: This paper consists of 18 MCQs, attempt all 18.
Correct answer or the most appropriate answer: Five marks (+5).
Any incorrect option marked will be given minus one mark (-1).
Unanswered/Marked for Review will be given no mark (0).
If more than one option is found to be correct then Five marks (+5) will be awarded to only those who have marked any of the correct options.
If all options are found to be correct then Five marks (+5) will be awarded to all those who have attempted the question.
If none of the options is found correct or a Question is found to be wrong or a Question is dropped then all candidates who have appeared will be given five marks (+5).
Calculator / any electronic gadgets are not permitted.

questions.question:
Arrange the following needs in sequence of priority as Maslow's Need Hierarchy Theory:
A. Physiological needs
B. Self actualisation needs
C. Safety needs
D. Belongingness needs

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: The hierarchy of needs as given by Maslow is: Basic physiological needs, safety needs, belongingness needs, esteem needs and self actualisation needs. From the given options, the correct ascending order of priority is A (Physiological) → C (Safety) → D (Belongingness) → B (Self-actualisation).

questions.question:
"This organisational structure has the disadvantage of increases in costs due to duplication of activities."

Identify the organisational structure being referred to here.
questions.explanation: Divisional Structure has various divisions made under one single organization. This increases the cost due to duplication of work, as each division maintains its own set of functions (marketing, finance, HR etc.), leading to redundancy.

questions.question:
The workers of a factory went on strike. To handle the issue, a meeting of Board of Directors of the company was called on urgent basis. The meeting continued for seven long hours. As a result, strike continued and productivity was zero.

Which limitation of planning is referred to in the above example/case?

options.A: Planning may not work in dynamic environment

questions.explanation: Planning is a very lengthy process as it consumes a lot of time for collection, analysis, and interpretation of data. Due to such a lengthy process, sometimes decisions get delayed and opportunities are lost. In this case, the seven-hour meeting delayed resolution of the strike, resulting in zero productivity.

questions.question: Rohan is running a small business of producing homemade craft items. He believes that since the business is small, he does not require 'management'. Which feature of management has been neglected?
questions.explanation: Management is required in all types of organizations whether it is political, social, cultural or business because it helps and directs various efforts towards a definite purpose. Rohan has neglected the fact that management is all pervasive — it is needed even in small businesses.

questions.question: "The effect of management is noticeable in an organisation where targets are met according to plans, employees are happy and satisfied, and there is orderliness instead of chaos." Which characteristic of management is highlighted here?
questions.explanation: Management is an intangible force, as it does not have any physical appearance. It can be felt by the way an organisation functions — through orderliness, employee satisfaction, and targets being met — even though management itself cannot be seen or touched.

questions.question: Identify the statement which is not the meaning of 'planning function' of management?

options.A: Deciding in advance what to do, how to do and when to do.
options.C: It seeks to bridge the gap between where we are and where we want to go.
options.D: It gives shape to the organisational structure

questions.correct_option_text: It gives shape to the organisational structure
questions.explanation: Span of management gives shape to the organisational structure, not planning. Span of management refers to the number of subordinates that can be effectively managed by the superiors. Options A, B and C are all valid meanings/characteristics of the planning function.

questions.question:
Match List-I with List-II:
List-I (Part-A):
A. Apprenticeship
B. Casual Caller
C. Promotion
D. Selection

List-II (Part-B):
I. Internal source of recruitment
II. Negative Process
III. Training
IV. External Sources

options.A: A - I, B - II, C - III, D - IV
options.B: A - II, B - IV, C - III, D - I
options.C: A - III, B - IV, C - II, D - II
options.D: A - III, B - I, C - II, D - IV

questions.correct_option_text: A - III, B - IV, C - II, D - II
questions.explanation: Apprenticeship is a method of training (III). Casual Caller is an external source of recruitment (IV). Promotion is an internal source and also considered a negative process of selection as it involves rejecting many (II). Selection is a negative process as more candidates are rejected than selected (II). Note: The original answer key marks Option (C) as correct.
questions.source_note: Option C has D mapped to 'II' which appears twice — the answer key provided marks Option (C) as correct with explanation listed as 'Solution not given' in the source PDF.

questions.question:
Match List-I with List-II:
List-I (Part-A):
A. Employment Interview
B. Employment Exchange
C. Internship Training
D. Vestibule Training

List-II (Part-B):
I. Source of recruitment
II. Collaboration of business firm and educational institutions
III. Formal, in-depth conversation
IV. Related to handling sophisticated machinery and equipment

options.A: A - I, B - II, C - IV, D - III
options.B: A - III, B - I, C - II, D - IV
options.C: A - II, B - III, C - I, D - IV
options.D: A - IV, B - I, C - III, D - II

questions.correct_option_text: A - III, B - I, C - II, D - IV
questions.explanation: Employment Interview is a formal, in-depth conversation (III) used to assess candidates. Employment Exchange is a government-run source of recruitment (I). Internship Training involves collaboration of business firms and educational institutions (II). Vestibule Training is related to handling sophisticated machinery and equipment (IV), conducted in a simulated work environment.

questions.question: It refers to the learning opportunities designed to help employees grow:
questions.explanation: Development refers to the learning opportunities designed to help employees grow. It is a career-oriented process that prepares employees for future roles beyond their current job responsibilities, unlike training which is job-specific and skill-focused.

questions.question: When Lenovo India decided to strengthen its diversity numbers, it decided to tap into its referral system. The company asked its workers to refer candidates and decided to reward those who become successful in this task. Identify the source of recruitment referred to here:
questions.explanation: Employee referrals are an excellent means of locating potential candidates for first hand to fill vacant positions. When existing employees recommend candidates and are rewarded for successful referrals, this is classified as 'Recommendation of Employees' — an external source of recruitment.

questions.question: Identify the process by which the aptitudes, skills and abilities of employees to perform specific jobs are increased.
questions.explanation: Training is a process by which the aptitudes, skills and abilities of employees to perform specific jobs are increased. Unlike development which is career-oriented and broad, training is job-specific and focused on improving performance in the current role.

questions.question:
Which of the following are advantages of branding to a marketer?
A. Enables making product differentiation
B. Helps in protecting the product
C. Helps in advertising and displaying programmes
D. To charge different price for its products different from the charged by its competitors
E. Help in product identification

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: Branding enables a marketer to make product differentiation (A) — distinguishing their product from competitors' — and helps in protecting the product (B) from imitation or counterfeiting through trademarks. While branding aids advertising, the other options (C, D, E) are not direct advantages of branding specifically to a marketer.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an element of delegation?
questions.explanation: There are three elements of Delegation: Assignment of Responsibility, Grant of Authority, and Creation of Accountability. Departmentalisation is a step in the process of organising (grouping similar activities into departments), not an element of delegation.

questions.question:
Identify the correct functions of entrepreneur in relation to economic development.
A. Capital Formation
B. Improvement in Economic Efficiency
C. Identification of specific product offering
D. Opportunity Scouting
E. Generation of Employment

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: The roles of entrepreneurs in economic development include: Capital Formation (A), Improvement in Per Capita Income/Economic Efficiency (B), Generation of Employment (E), Balanced Regional Development, Improvement in Living Standards, and Economic Independence. Identification of specific product offering (C) and Opportunity Scouting (D) are entrepreneurial activities but not standard economic development functions listed in the syllabus.
questions.subtopic: Role of Entrepreneur in Economic Development

questions.question:
Mr. Vivek opened the Geyser box and found a booklet containing information about the geyser.

Identify the consumer right highlighted in the above statement.
questions.explanation: The right to be informed is defined as the right to be informed about the quality, quantity, potency, purity, standard and price of goods or services. The booklet inside the Geyser box containing product information is an exercise of the manufacturer's obligation to inform consumers, corresponding to the consumer's right to be informed.

questions.question: Under which consumer right does a business firm set up consumer grievance cell?
questions.explanation: Under the right to be heard, business firms set up consumer grievance cells. This right ensures that consumer interests receive due consideration and that consumers have the right to be heard in proceedings affecting them, including having their complaints addressed through grievance mechanisms.

questions.question:
Fill in the blank:

The earlier approach of caveat emptor, which means "Let the buyer beware", has now been changed to ________, which means, "Let the seller beware".
questions.explanation: Caveat venditor is a Latin term meaning 'let the seller beware'. It is the modern shift from the old principle of caveat emptor ('let the buyer beware'), which placed all responsibility on the buyer. The Consumer Protection Act reflects this shift — placing responsibility on the seller to ensure product quality and safety.

questions.question: Which of the following cannot file a complaint under Consumer Protection Act?
questions.explanation: A Registered Traders' Association cannot file a complaint under the Consumer Protection Act. The Act allows complaints to be filed by: a consumer, any voluntary consumer association registered under any law, the Central or State Government, one or more consumers on behalf of others, and legal heirs or representatives of a deceased consumer. Traders' associations are not given this right as they represent sellers, not consumers.
